calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-09-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Nevis for (personal representative) Joel Mikazes.

The Court confirmed that Mr. Nevis was not anticipating anyone else, that we are now procedurally able to go forward, that there are concerns, that it is not approving all of the actions of the administrator, that it is not inclined to approve the administrators assignment of interest that he did not have in the estate and that in regards to Kemp & Associates asks why should the Court approve control when they are not entitled to it. Mr. Nevis responded to the early distribution giving a history of what Alfred Mikazes did, that Joel is Alfred's son but was not aware of what had been done until last year, that all of the beneficiaries were informed and that while the beneficiaries did not approve of what was done they are not intending to litigate. The Court stated that it cannot approve it, that it was done improperly and that it is not ordering relief. Mr. Nevis stated that Joel's assignment was improper and will have to be worked out with the creditor, that in regards to Kemp, Mr. Nevis shares the concerns of the Court, that he has talked to the beneficiaries and counsel for Kemp, that they say they will provide receipts, that he was instructed to make out the distribution checks to the beneficiaries but to send them to Kemp, that he has the beneficiaries consent on this and that Kemp will turn over the distributions to the beneficiaries. The Court stated that it is not sure why it should be a party to this, that it is not clear why Kemp should be given the distribution and that it is not proper. Mr. Nevis suggested issuing checks directly to the beneficiaries for their 85% interest and checks to Kemp for the remaining 15%. The Court stated that there is another issue raised by the examiner as to whether the $542.00 reduction in attorney's fees is sufficient response. Mr. Nevis stated that whatever the Court believes is appropriate will not be objected to, that they had deposited the funds into his trust account as they thought the estate would be handled quickly. The Court took the accounting petition and the petition for allowance of compensation for extraordinary services under submission stating that it will be revising the order and that it is inclined towards distribution of 15% to Kemp and 85% to the beneficiaries.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Allowance Of Compensation For Extraordinary Services
Off Calendar Jun-09-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Allowance Of Compensation For Extraordinary Services
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Continued To May-31-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Continued To May-31-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Nevis for (petitioner) Joel Mikazes.

The Court stated that this is a second amended petition for final distribution and a petition for extraordinary fees, that it is not prepared to go forward, that the petition needs to be amended regarding the heir hunter assignments and that the administrator needs to ask for a result. Mr. Nevis stated that the administrator does have a position. The Court stated that it needs to be addressed, that the court also has concerns about why the settlement proceeds were left in an attorney trust fund, that the petitioner paid money to himself which the court is not sure will be approved, that it is not appropriate to approve assignment of estate assets for a non-beneficiary, and that an amended petition with appropriate notice is necessary. The accounting petition and the petition for allowance of compensation for extraordinary services were continued to May 31, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Oct-07-2014 Continued To Dec-16-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Nevis and Jordan Ryan for Joel Mikazes. Michael Carlson for Casa Sanchez Company/Corporation.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. Mr. Carslon requested 90-days to conduct discovery. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than December 12, 2014. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by December 12, 2014. The Petition to Establish Estate's Claim of Ownership to Property, etc. was continued to and the Parties are to return on December 16, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
